zh.lifeSummary: Teresa Fernández de Traba是Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara和Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal的女兒，出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安利安利安。她於大約1176年與Ferdinand II de León King of Galicia and結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1180年2月6日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安帕倫希亞杜埃尼亞斯。

nameMeanings.name: Fernández
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Jorge, Miguel, Fernando, Mario, Jesus, Pedro, Francisco.  Portuguese Ligia, Joao, Armanda, Godofredo, Wenceslao.

Spanish (Fernández): patronymic from the personal name Fernando . The surname (and to a lesser extent the variant Hernandez ) has also been established in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some Jewish descendants retained the surname that their families had adopted as Roman Catholics.

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Orlando, Aida, Carlos, Jesusa, Rodolfo, Ruben, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.  Indian Rohan, Lalith, Nimal, Rajiv, Sanjeeva, Shanthi, Anil, Anusha, Dushan, Kapila, Lakshman, Mallika.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Fernando, a younger form of Ferdinando (see Ferdinand ). This surname is also found in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there, and as a result of the expulsion of the Jews from Spain and Portugal at the end of the 15th century, many of whom moved to Italy. It is also common in western India and especially in Sri Lanka, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Traba
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Francesco, Gasper, Antonio, Armando, Biagio, Emanuele, Gustavo, Marco, Nicola, Santo.

nameMeanings.name: Teresa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Theresa .  In the English-speaking world the name is often chosen in this spelling by Roman Catholics, with particular reference to the Spanish saint, Teresa of Ávila (  Teresa Cepeda de Ahumada ,  1515–82 ).

variants.name: Theresa
meanings: Of problematic origin. The name seems to have been first used in Spain and Portugal, and, according to tradition, was the name of the wife of St Paulinus of Nola, who spent most of his life in Spain; she was said to have originated (and to have derived her name) from the Greek island of Thēra. However, this story is neither factually nor etymologically confirmed.

variants.name: Teresia
meanings: form of   Theresa .  In the English-speaking world the name is often chosen in this spelling by Roman Catholics, with particular reference to the Spanish saint, Teresa of Ávila (  Teresa Cepeda de Ahumada ,  1515–82 ).

en.lifeSummary: Teresa Fernández de Traba was born in León, León, León, Castilla y León, Spain as the daughter of Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara and Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal. She married Ferdinand II de León King of Galicia and about 1176. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 6 February 1180, in León, Castilla y León, Spain, and was buried in Dueñas, Palencia, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Teresa Fernández de Traba est née à León, León, León, Castilla y León, Espagne, fille de Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara et Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal. Elle a épousé Ferdinand II de León King of Galicia and environ 1176. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 6 février 1180, à León, Castilla y León, Espagne, et a été enterrée à Dueñas, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espagne.

ko.lifeSummary: Teresa Fernández de Traba(은)는 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 레o 레o 레o에서 Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara(와)과 Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1176년 Ferdinand II de León King of Galicia and(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1180년 2월 6일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 레o에서 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 파렌샤 드n아스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Teresa Fernández de Traba nasceu em León, León, León, Castilla y León, Espanha filha de Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara e Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal. Ela casou-se com Ferdinand II de León King of Galicia and aproximadamente 1176. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 6 de fevereiro de 1180, em León, Castilla y León, Espanha, e foi sepultada em Dueñas, Palencia, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Teresa Fernández de Traba родилась в местности леoн, леoн, леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания. Её родителей звали Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara и Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ferdinand II de León King of Galicia and около 1176 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 6 февраля 1180 г. в местности леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания и была похоронена в местности дуеnас, пеиленся, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Teresa Fernández de Traba nació en León, León, León, Castilla y León, España como hija de Conde Fernando Pérez de Traba de Trastámara y Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal. Se casó con Ferdinand II de León King of Galicia and aproximadamente 1176. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 6 de febrero de 1180, en León, Castilla y León, España, y fue sepultada en Dueñas, Palencia, Castilla y León, España.
